Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings Or Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT EAZE TECHNOLOGIES, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings Or Motion For Protective Order

Defendant Eaze Technologies' Notice of Motion and Motion to Stay Proceedings or Motion for Protective Order is DENIED. Preliminarily, while the Motion was not served on sufficient days, the Court finds that Plaintiff had sufficient time to oppose the Motion and is not prejudiced as the hearing was continued from 9/23 to 10/16. As to the merits, the Motion provides no basis to stay the proceedings. The Court agrees with Plaintiff's counsel's argument that "When and if EAZE files bankruptcy, a stay will be imposed." (Opp., 4:9.)

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in coplaince with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti at peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (525/JPT)

Motion To Compel Answer To Form Interrogatory 17.1 And For Sanctions And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-16-2024 Text Ruling

CROSS DEFENDANT SUPERIOR HERBAL HEALTH, LLC DBA THE CLINIK LA, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S Motion To Compel Answer To Form Interrogatory 17.1 And For Sanctions And Costs

Plaintiff's unopposed Motion to Compel Answer to FI 17.1 is GRANTED. Defendant shall serve verified, amended responses to FI 17.1 as to RFAs Nos. 15, 21 (mistakenly designated as No. 20), No. 25, (mistakenly designated as No 26), No 32, (mistakenly identified as No 31), and No. 33 (mistakenly identified as No. 32) within two weeks of notice of entry of this order. All requests for sanctions are denied.

The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in coplaince with CRC 3.1312 and must e-mail it to Judge Pro Tem Peter Catalanotti at peter.catalanotti@wilsonelser.com.
